---
title: "Introduction"
---

## An educational research project

BROWT is, above all, an **educational research project**. The goal is not a
finished tool but to study — hands on — the hard problems behind an agent that
plays Brawl Stars from the screen alone, with no access to the game's internal
state.

Two problems are deliberately pushed in depth, because they are the most
challenging and the most instructive.

### 1. Perception — detecting objects in the frame

Identifying the relevant objects (brawlers, gems, ...) in each frame. Public
datasets for this game are **limited**, so a large part of the work is studying
and comparing **techniques that push detection accuracy as far as possible**,
with **all training and inference kept local** — no cloud services.

### 2. Decision — acting on unlabeled information

Choosing what to do from the perceived state, **without labels** telling us the
correct action. This is a **reinforcement-learning** problem: the agent must
learn a policy from its own experience and a reward signal.

## How this documentation is organized

The documentation mirrors these two axes:

1. **Detection and analysis** — the perception problem above.
2. **Reinforcement learning** — the decision problem above.

Each part is split into its own sections, added as the work progresses. A final
part presents the **general pipeline** that ties everything together —
deliberately placed last, once both halves are understood.
